narrative.headline: 2022 Chevrolet Suburban: engine leads owner complaints
narrative.summary: Across 102 owner complaints to NHTSA, the engine is the most-reported area at 50 reports (49%), nearly half of all complaints and well ahead of the power train (28, 27%) and electrical system (18, 18%). The electrical system carries the highest harm count: 1 crash and 3 injuries. The seat belts category, the smallest at 6 reports, notes 1 crash and 1 fire. No deaths are reported in any category.

issues.title: Engine
issues.blurb: Most-reported area: 50 complaints (49%), nearly half of all reports, with no crashes, fires, or injuries. One owner describes the engine sounding low on oil and finding it about 2 quarts low despite regular oil changes.

issues.sample: When I started up the vehicle with the key fab button I noticed the cabin air blower came on as normal however when we went to the cockpit we noticed the engine was not running. When we went to start the vehicle (holding break and pressing start button) the dashboard started the
issues.title: Power Train
issues.blurb: 28 complaints (27%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. One owner describes starting the vehicle and finding the engine not running even though the cabin blower came on as normal.

issues.sample: When I started up the vehicle with the key fab button I noticed the cabin air blower came on as normal however when we went to the cockpit we noticed the engine was not running. When we went to start the vehicle (holding break and pressing start button) the dashboard started the
issues.title: Electrical System
issues.blurb: 18 complaints (18%), and the highest harm of any category here: 1 crash and 3 injuries. One owner describes the engine failing to run at startup even though the cabin blower came on as normal.

issues.title: Fuel/Propulsion System
issues.blurb: 10 complaints (10%), with no crashes, fires, or injuries reported. One owner describes starting the vehicle and finding the engine not running despite the cabin blower coming on.

issues.title: Seat Belts
issues.blurb: Smallest category at 6 complaints (6%), but it notes 1 crash and 1 fire. One owner describes a roughly 30 mph crash into another vehicle's driver-side door, which was then pushed into a third vehicle.
